summary:
Sternal plating, particularly in high-risk patients, has been associated with improved bone healing, reduced pain, enhanced upper-body function, and fewer episodes of mediastinitis due to greater mechanical stability. Evidence suggests a clinically meaningful advantage in select patient populations despite limitations in large-scale randomized trial data.  

Final answer: Yes – in high-risk patients. \boxed{\text{A}}.
